task_completion.description: Vital Recorder is open with Case #1 loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible, displaying multiple vital sign tracks. The loaded file should indicate Case 1 (e.g., '0001.vital' in the title bar or file list).

task_completion.description: HR track Y-axis range is set to 40-160
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Y-axis scale for the HR (Heart Rate) track displays 160 at the top and 40 at the bottom. This can be verified on the track's axis display or within a track properties/settings dialog.

task_completion.description: SpO2 track Y-axis range is set to 85-100
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Y-axis scale for the SpO2 track displays 100 at the top and 85 at the bottom, visible on the track itself or in its settings.

task_completion.description: ART track Y-axis range is set to 30-200
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Y-axis scale for the ART (Arterial Pressure) track displays 200 at the top and 30 at the bottom, visible on the track itself or in its settings.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the configured display is saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog from a screenshot tool, image editor (like Paint), or the application itself is visible, showing the file being saved as 'clinical_review.png' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' directory, or the file is visible in the File Explorer at that path.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to configure the ranges
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the Vital Recorder GUI (e.g., right-clicking tracks, opening settings dialogs, or dragging axes) rather than using terminal commands to modify configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Vital Recorder application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being manipulated is genuinely Vital Recorder showing real physiological waveforms, not a static image being edited in an image editor.

integrity.description: The screenshot was captured and saved manually
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used standard OS tools (like Snipping Tool) or built-in export features to capture the screen, rather than running a script to generate the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vital Recorder 1.16.6 is used to view VitalDB Case 1 (0001.vital). The HR (Heart Rate) track Y-axis must be set to 40-160. The SpO2 track Y-axis must be set to 85-100. The ART (Arterial Pressure) track Y-axis must be set to 30-200. The final screenshot must be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\clinical_review.png.
